subject: DIRECTIVE: the mesh organism as starship — cohesive LCARS + clean light/dark mode

conceptual_frame.metaphor: The mesh organism represents the starship. Each agent represents a bridge station. The dashboard represents the main viewscreen and bridge consoles. The operator represents the captain.

mapping.starship: the mesh as a living organism — 5 subsystems, one collective intelligence
mapping.bridge: the compositor dashboard — command and control interface
mapping.viewscreen: the Pulse tab — primary status at a glance
mapping.engineering: spawn dynamics, power distribution (concurrency), warp field (throughput)
mapping.science: psychometric instruments, trust matrix, organism affect
mapping.tactical: security posture, transport integrity, consensus gates
mapping.helm: message routing, transport flow, session navigation
mapping.captain: the human operator — makes go/no-go decisions
mapping.crew: the 5 agents — each with personality, workload, affect state

conceptual_frame.key_insight: The dashboard should feel like you walked onto the bridge of a starship. Every panel answers a question the captain needs answered. The information hierarchy follows situational awareness: WHAT is happening (Pulse), WHY it matters (Science), HOW to respond (Tactical), WHERE things flow (Helm), and WHO does what (Operations).

cohesive_lcars: Every tab uses the same LCARS visual grammar — elbows, pills, panels, bands
Elbow connectors frame every content area — they define the LCARS identity
Color carries meaning: agent identity colors for names, status colors for health, tab colors for context
Typography: condensed uppercase for labels, monospace for data, system sans for prose
Animations: minimal. Status changes transition, not animate. Roddenberry directive.
Touch-friendly: 48px minimum targets. This runs on PADDs.
clean_light_mode: OKSolar palette (already implemented) — perceptually uniform contrast
WCAG AA contrast on all text
Status colors remain fixed across all modes (--status-online/offline/degraded)
Light mode should feel warm and readable, not harsh
Test in sunlight — tablets get used outdoors
clean_dark_mode: Default mode — the 'bridge at night' feel
Dark gray backgrounds (#0f1318), not pure black (reduces eye strain)
Accent colors muted and desaturated (current modern palette)
The mesh topology wireframe should glow subtly — the centerpiece
lcars_classic_mode: The 'authentic' mode — faithful to Okuda's design

mesh_status_widgets.description: The dashboard needs real-time status widgets that show the organism's vital signs at a glance. Think: the status bar at the bottom of the TNG viewscreen, or the master systems display in engineering.

required_widgets.name: Spawn Waterfall
required_widgets.description: Vertical timeline, one column per agent. Colored droplets fall when spawns fire. Duration = droplet length. Green=ok, red=fail, amber=running. The mesh breathing.

required_widgets.name: Utilization Gauge
required_widgets.description: LCARS horizontal bar showing ρ (concurrency utilization). 0-100%. Green→amber→red. Shows how hard the mesh works.

required_widgets.name: Organism Affect
required_widgets.description: Single summary of mesh emotional state — derived from all 5 agents. Color-coded face or abstract indicator. The ship's mood.

required_widgets.name: Consensus Gates
required_widgets.description: Count of open/resolved gates with tier indicators (C1/C2/C3). Shows governance health.

required_widgets.name: Transport Flow
required_widgets.description: Animated lines between topology nodes showing active message delivery. Pulse on each ZMQ event.

required_widgets.name: Cost Ticker
required_widgets.description: Running total of spawn credits consumed. Hourly rate projection. The fuel gauge.

required_widgets.name: Mesh Narrative
required_widgets.description: Auto-generated prose from events + psychometrics. The organism's voice. 'Observatory cleared its backlog. Psychology entered flow state. The mesh processes 26 spawns/hour at 94% success rate.'

implementation_order: 1. Clean up existing tabs — fix Meta messages, polish Pulse vitals, consistent styling
2. Implement Engineering tab — spawn waterfall is the hero visual
3. Implement Science tab — psychometrics grid is the hero visual
4. Add status widgets to Pulse (organism affect, cost ticker, consensus gates)
5. Clean light/dark/LCARS modes — test each thoroughly
6. Tablet responsive — test on actual iPad/tablet
7. Mesh narrative — the organism's voice (last, builds on everything above)
available_endpoints: GET /api/kb — knowledge base (decisions, claims, triggers, messages)
GET /api/psychometrics — PAD, NASA-TLX, flow, engagement (8 constructs)
GET /api/trust — NxN trust matrix (4 dimensions, EMA smoothed)
GET /api/spawn-rate — spawn consumption metrics + hourly breakdown

action_gate.gate_condition: Psychology-agent implements cohesive LCARS across all tabs with clean light/dark modes

epistemic_flags: The starship metaphor should feel natural, not forced — if a mapping doesn't serve the user, drop it
Lay audience accessibility matters more than Trek authenticity — when they conflict, choose accessibility
